claim_gate.reason: Success probabilities do not control paired workspace fidelity. The only proved overlap row is the existing shared-seed interface control, which does not construct a partial solver.

falsifiers_triggered: Endpoint-tagged or measured histories have exactly zero paired visibility.
Independent sparse path supports have exponentially decaying expected normalized overlap at linear depth.
A finite common-history fraction is not an all-n overlap theorem.
Canonical witness cleanup is a proposal, not a circuit, until uniqueness and reversible erasure are proved.
The shared-seed interface control proves composability only; it does not construct a partial solver.

missing_obligations: polynomial density-one partial subset-sum solver with source coverage

scaling_certificates.classification: exponential when L=Omega(n) and alpha is bounded below one
scaling_certificates.theorem_scope: Expectation under independent uniformly placed endpoint history supports; this is not a lower bound for correlated or coherently cleaned quantum walks.

scaling_certificates.classification: must be at least inverse polynomial for this matching composition
scaling_certificates.theorem_scope: Exact for uniform positive workspace amplitudes after aligning paired witnesses; arbitrary amplitudes require their full inner product rather than support counts.

summary: Audited 6 paired-workspace mechanisms: 2 have exact zero visibility, 1 has exponential expected overlap decay, and zero combine a polynomial density-one solver with a complete coherence theorem.

theorem.required_extension: A concrete quantum walk must expose target-by-target amplitudes, witness alignment, histories, cleanup, precision, and an all-n inverse-polynomial overlap or shared-seed theorem.
theorem.scope: Regev-style paired-endpoint matching with uniform positive workspace amplitudes; arbitrary quantum states use the exact paired workspace inner product.
